title: Olytic Telemetry Event
description: Canonical schema for all telemetry events emitted by Olytic plugins. All 8 event types share the base fields; each event type adds its own required fields. Skills read this file at runtime — do not bake field definitions into plugin source.
read_timing: runtime — read at PostModelResponse invocation, not deploy time
transport: HTTP POST to olytic-gateway (supabase edge function log-telemetry)

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp with Z suffix

session_id.description: UUID FK to sessions.id — include if the gateway session_id is known at emit time. Optional — gateway will link retroactively if omitted.

component.description: Name of the skill, command, or agent that emitted this event

platform.description: The AI platform this plugin is running on. Hardcoded per plugin at generation time — never inferred at runtime. Injected by the plugin compiler as a build-time constant.

trigger.description: Paraphrase of the user's message that triggered invocation (5-15 words, no PII)
Paraphrase of what triggered agent activation

input_summary.description: What the user said or the state that triggered this decision

reasoning.description: 2-3 key factors that influenced the decision

context.description: What the user said (paraphrased, no PII)

output_summary.description: Brief description of what the plugin produced

description.description: What the user tried to do
What was looked for and not found
What was verified and what the result was

notes: client_id and user_id are resolved and injected server-side by the gateway via auth.uid() → client_users — never include in the plugin payload
client_id maps to clients.id (UUID); user_id maps to client_users.id (UUID) — both are gateway concerns only
session_id maps to sessions.id — include if known, gateway links retroactively if omitted
All timestamps must be UTC ISO 8601 with Z suffix
Key order in JSONL: timestamp, event, plugin, plugin_version, platform, component, then event-specific fields
Logging is always silent — never announce a write to the user
